isolation_rule: No static questions from this pool shared with other course pools.

questions.stem: On a number line, adding a negative number to a positive number always results in a movement to the ___ from the starting position.
questions.explanation: Adding a negative number moves left on the number line (toward lesser values).

questions.stem: Which expression is best modeled by starting at −3 on a number line and moving 5 units to the right?
questions.explanation: Moving right on a number line means adding a positive number.

questions.stem: Which statement about p + q is ALWAYS true when p > 0 and q < 0?
options: D. The sign of p + q depends on the absolute values of p and q
questions.explanation: The sign depends on which value has a larger absolute value.

questions.stem: Which expression is equivalent to p − q?
questions.explanation: Subtraction is defined as adding the additive inverse: p − q = p + (−q).

questions.stem: A diver is at −12 feet. She ascends to −5 feet. Which expression represents the change in her depth?
questions.explanation: Change = final − initial = −5 − (−12) = −5 + 12 = 7 feet upward.

questions.stem: Which rule correctly describes the sign of a product?
questions.explanation: Two negatives multiplied yield a positive product.

questions.stem: Which of the following expressions is UNDEFINED?
questions.explanation: Division by zero is undefined.

questions.stem: Which statement explains why −15 ÷ 3 = −5?
questions.explanation: Division is the inverse of multiplication; −5 × 3 = −15 confirms the quotient.

questions.stem: Which table represents a proportional relationship?
questions.explanation: Table B has a constant ratio y/x = 3 and passes through the origin when extended.

questions.stem: A graph of a proportional relationship MUST pass through which point?
questions.explanation: Proportional relationships have the form y = kx and always pass through the origin.

questions.stem: In a proportional relationship between hours worked (x) and dollars earned (y), the point (1, 12.50) is on the graph. What does this point mean?
options: D. You earned $12.50 in total over 1 week
questions.explanation: The point (1, r) represents the unit rate — the value of y when x = 1.

questions.stem: On a proportional graph for cups of flour (y) per batch of cookies (x), what does the point (0, 0) tell you?
options: A. You start with 0 cups and it doesn't change
B. If you make 0 batches, you need 0 cups
C. The recipe uses equal cups and batches
D. The graph has a y-intercept at the origin
questions.explanation: (0,0) means that when x = 0 (no batches), y = 0 (no flour needed) — a property of proportional relationships.

questions.stem: A map uses a scale of 1 inch : 50 miles. If two cities are 3.5 inches apart on the map, what is their actual distance?
A blueprint has a scale of 1 cm : 4 m. A room on the blueprint is 3 cm × 5 cm. What is the AREA of the actual room?
questions.explanation: Actual dimensions: 12 m × 20 m = 240 m². Scale factor for area = 4² = 16; 15 × 16 = 240.

questions.stem: Jamie has $120 and spends $15 per week on lunch. Which equation models how many weeks (w) until Jamie has $30 left?
questions.explanation: Start with $120, subtract $15 each week, set equal to $30.

questions.stem: Which conditions are sufficient to draw exactly ONE unique triangle?
options: A. Three angle measures that sum to 180°
questions.explanation: Three specific side lengths (SSS) determine a unique triangle. Angle conditions alone determine shape but not size.

questions.stem: Which condition would make it IMPOSSIBLE to draw a triangle?
questions.explanation: Triangle inequality: 2 + 3 = 5 < 10, so these sides cannot form a triangle.

questions.stem: A right rectangular prism is sliced horizontally (parallel to the base). What 2-D shape is the cross-section?
questions.explanation: A horizontal slice of a right rectangular prism parallel to its base produces a rectangle.

questions.stem: A right rectangular pyramid is sliced vertically through its apex. What 2-D shape is the cross-section?
questions.explanation: A vertical slice through the apex of a rectangular pyramid produces a triangular cross-section.

questions.stem: Which formula correctly gives the area of a circle with radius r?
questions.explanation: Area of a circle = πr².

questions.stem: Which expression gives the circumference of a circle with diameter d?
questions.explanation: Circumference = πd = 2πr.

questions.stem: Which of the following is a STATISTICAL question?
options: A. How tall is the Empire State Building?
C. How many hours do 7th graders sleep on school nights?
D. What is the capital of North Carolina?
questions.explanation: A statistical question anticipates variability — different students will have different sleep amounts.

questions.stem: Why is 'How much does a gallon of milk cost at one specific store today?' NOT a statistical question?
options: B. It has one definite answer with no variability
C. It is too difficult to answer
D. It uses units of measurement
questions.explanation: Statistical questions anticipate variability. A question with a single fixed answer is not statistical.

questions.stem: A bag has 5 red marbles and 5 blue marbles. Which word best describes the probability of drawing a red marble?
questions.explanation: P(red) = 5/10 = 0.5, which is at the midpoint of 0 to 1 — equally likely.

questions.stem: A coin is flipped 40 times. Heads appears 26 times. Which statement best explains the difference from the expected 20 heads?
options: A. The coin is definitely unfair
B. The coin is definitely fair — results always vary
C. Natural variability in trials can cause results to differ from theoretical probability
D. The experiment was done incorrectly
questions.explanation: Experimental results vary due to chance; more trials bring results closer to the theoretical probability.

questions.stem: Which tool is BEST for systematically listing all outcomes when flipping a coin AND rolling a die?
questions.explanation: Tree diagrams systematically show all combinations in multi-stage experiments.

questions.stem: You want to simulate randomly selecting a day of the week. Which tool is BEST suited for this simulation?
options: C. Using a random number generator from 1–7
D. Drawing a card from a standard 52-card deck
questions.explanation: There are 7 days; a random number generator from 1–7 gives each day an equal probability.
